text: Used for Organization/Person JSON-LD and sameAs profile links. Leave fields empty if the identity node should stay minimal.
JSON defaults are resolved before page-level values. Use field:title, field:summary|truncate:160, custom text, or inherit.
Use typed source values like field:title, field:summary|truncate:160, custom text, or inherit.
By default Ichiban only renders tags when your template outputs $page->seo. Automatic injection is opt-in.
Automatically inject SEO tags into page <head>
Enable only if your templates do not output $page->seo.
Optionally format the rendered <title>. Use {meta_title} for the resolved page title, for example {meta_title} | {site_name}. Leave blank to render the title unchanged.
Supported placeholders: {meta_title}, {site_name}, {entity_name}, {host}. Title length checks include this format.
Disable this when ProcessWire languages are used internally but the public site should not advertise language alternates.
Disable this when templates or another module generate custom structured data.

Verification tags and Meta Pixel are rendered on the home page only. Google Search Console verification is intentionally omitted here because current Google setup should use DNS TXT or CNAME verification.
Microsoft Bing, Yahoo, and DuckDuckGo visibility often starts from Bing Webmaster Tools.
Numeric Pixel ID. Renders the standard PageView pixel on the home page.
One per line as meta-name=token. Use for regional tools that provide a meta verification tag.
Example: example-site-verification=abc123. Only simple meta names are rendered.

Enable the Google Search Console API, create an OAuth Web application client, add this authorized redirect URI: %s, publish the OAuth app to production, then paste the Client ID and Client Secret below. Existing tokens are preserved when settings are saved.
Access token expired; refresh token is saved.
Enter the Search Console domain, for example lqrs.uk. Ichiban will query it as a Domain property. You can still enter a URL-prefix property like https://example.com/ when needed.

Moz Links API is used by the Backlinks section for inbound links, linking root domains, and anchor text. Use the new Moz API token when possible; legacy Access ID and Secret Key are only a fallback. Start with a small row limit to control monthly usage.
Used by the Backlinks preview tables.
OpenRouter connection used by the Ichiban AI section. This follows the same OpenAI-compatible pattern as the Context module: provider, API key, model, limits, and OpenRouter attribution headers.
OpenRouter format: provider/model, for example anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 or openai/gpt-4o-mini.
You are a helpful SEO assistant for ProcessWire websites.

IndexNow keys are simple verification strings, not keys from an account. Generate one here or use any 8-128 character key containing letters, numbers, and hyphens. A public key file named {key}.txt must exist at the site root and contain the key.
Used when Ichiban pings IndexNow after a public page is saved.
Click Save to write/update the public key file if the site root is writable.
Controls canonical, og:url, twitter:url, and hreflang on pages that use ProcessWire URL segments.
Public index: %s. Use the Sitemap tab for generation status and manual actions.
Relative to the ProcessWire web root.
One substring or regex per line.
One CSV row per URL: loc,lastmod,changefreq,priority. JSON array is also accepted.

RobotsTxt companion module is installed. Use Setup > Robots.txt for the physical robots.txt editor and presets, or enable Ichiban robots.txt only when you want Ichiban to serve it dynamically. Avoid managing robots.txt in both places at once.

Install the companion RobotsTxt module if you want a full physical robots.txt editor with presets, rules overview, file status, and a View file action. Ichiban can still serve a simple dynamic robots.txt when enabled below.
Ichiban appends Sitemap automatically when no Sitemap line is present.
Comma-separated template names. Leave empty for all published pages.
One Markdown link or plain URL per line.
Use cleanup options carefully on production. Blocked search queries are recorded in the Cleanup log.
A quick overview of metadata health, crawl cleanup activity, redirects, and recent SEO changes.
Pages that may share poorly on social networks.
Published pages hidden from search engines.
No redirects have been created yet.
Index not built yet. Please run Audit → Rebuild Index first.
Edit meta titles and descriptions across indexed pages without opening each page individually.
Changes are saved as custom SEO values for each page.
No indexed pages match this filter
Review changed fields, then save all visible edits together.
%d submitted pages were skipped because they no longer have an Ichiban SEO field.
Index not built yet. Please rebuild the index first.
Every page must have a meta title. Missing titles prevent search engines from identifying page content.
Meta title should be 30–70 characters. Too short lacks context; too long gets truncated in search results.
Each page should have a unique meta title. Duplicate titles confuse search engines about which page to rank.
Meta description should be set on every page. It appears as the snippet in search results and affects click-through rate.
Meta description should be 50–160 characters. Longer descriptions get cut off in search results.
Each page should have a unique meta description. Duplicates reduce the value of individual pages in search rankings.
Open Graph image should be set so shared links look rich on social media.
Canonical URL must be an absolute URL starting with http. Invalid canonicals can cause indexing issues.
Published pages should not have noindex set unless intentionally hidden from search engines.
URLs should use hyphens instead of underscores. Google treats hyphens as word separators, underscores as word joiners.
Schema.org type should be set to help search engines understand page content and enable rich results.
Review technical SEO signals, metadata coverage, social sharing readiness, and schema basics across indexed pages.
Rebuild the index after large content, template, or SEO field changes.
Manage manual, imported, and automatic redirects from one place.
Search by source, target, or note
Treat From as a regular expression pattern.
Allowed types: 301, 302, 307, 410, 451. Use 1 for regex patterns and 0 for plain paths.
Created automatically after a page path change.
Add Google OAuth Client ID and Client Secret in Settings before connecting Search Console.
Google Search Console disconnected. Cached Insights and Page Indexing scan data were cleared.
Security token expired. Reload the page and click Disconnect GSC again.
Checked %d URLs with URL Inspection API.
URL Inspection scan did not return results: %s
Security token expired. Reload the page and run Scan indexing issues again.
Review Google Search Console performance, page indexing checks, and search breakdowns from cached API data.
Moz quota lookup did not return data.
Monitor inbound links, linking domains, and anchor text through the Moz Links API.
Create or open a Moz API account.
During signup, Moz asks for a debit or credit card even on the free plan. This is part of their security and abuse-prevention flow.
Create an API token in the Moz API dashboard.
Paste the token into the Moz API token field in %s, then save.
Fallback: if token auth does not work for your account, open Show Legacy Credentials for the token and paste the Access ID and Secret Key instead.
Return here, keep the default 5-row limit, and click Refresh from Moz. The free plan quota is tiny, so avoid repeated refreshes.

Ichiban sends the new token as the x-moz-token header. Every refresh is saved as a backlink snapshot and reused from cache on page reload. Default API base URL: https://lsapi.seomoz.com/v2. Change it only if Moz gives you a different endpoint.
Click Refresh quota to save current Moz usage inside Ichiban.
Moz credentials are missing. Add your API token, or legacy Access ID and Secret Key, then return to this page to fetch backlink data.

After connecting, Ichiban will show Search Console metrics for 7, 28, 90, 180, and 365 days, including daily trends, top pages, top queries, countries, devices, search appearance, and cached Page Indexing scans.

Enter the exact Search Console property you use. For Domain properties, use only the bare domain, for example lqrs.uk. Ichiban automatically sends it to Google as a sc-domain property. Use https://example.com/ only for URL-prefix properties.
Create or choose a Google Cloud project.
Review tracked SEO field changes and restore previous values when a page edit needs to be rolled back.
Enter a valid recipient email address for the test.
Test report could not be sent. Check the selected WireMail provider and sender settings.
LazyCron will generate the report as JSON, store the latest snapshot here, and send the same report by email. DOCX export is available for printing or sharing with a client.
Email delivery is enabled in settings.
A short operational digest: current SEO score, new critical issues, pages that need attention, Search Console movement, and indexing warnings from the latest cached scan.
A broader trend summary: score change, issue totals, top winning and losing pages, query visibility, cleanup activity, redirect hits, and recommendations for the next month.
SEO health score and critical audit issues
Missing titles, descriptions, OG images, and noindex pages
Search Console clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position
Top pages, top queries, countries, devices, and search appearance
Recent redirects, cleanup blocks, and SEO revisions
No report JSON has been generated yet.
Save your settings first, then test. The email is sent through the currently selected WireMail provider.
PHP ZipArchive extension is required to generate DOCX reports.
Cleanup log table is not available yet.
Monitor spam search queries and lightweight crawl-surface cleanup that keeps low-value URLs out of public search paths.
Generate and inspect the XML sitemap files served by Ichiban.
Sitemap auto-regeneration is enabled, but LazyCron is not installed. Install the core LazyCron module or use Generate Now manually.
The sitemap directory does not exist yet.
The sitemap directory is not writable.
Repaired duplicate Ichiban fields. Kept %s, removed %s from %d template(s), copied %d row(s). Backups: %s.
Converted field %s. Backup table: %s. Rows migrated: %d.
Convert existing SeoMaestro fields to Ichiban without deleting the field or losing per-page SEO data.
FieldtypeIchiban is not installed yet. Install it from Modules before running a migration.
Create a backup and convert this field to Ichiban?
Field tokens like {title} become Ichiban field sources; custom text stays custom text.
Field tokens become sources, custom copy is preserved.
Copied as the page canonical override when it is not inherited.
Field tokens become sources for social title output.
Field tokens become sources for social description output.
Copied as the selected OG image source. Ichiban resolves image output at render time.
Copied as alternate text for the OG image.
Copied when SeoMaestro stored an explicit type.
Copied as the page Twitter/X card mode.
Copied as the page creator handle.
Copied only when the value is not inherit.
Copied only when the value is not inherit; otherwise Ichiban defaults to included.
Copied as the page-level sitemap priority.
Copied as the page-level change frequency.
This site has more than one FieldtypeIchiban field. Ichiban will keep %s as the canonical SEO field and can remove %s from templates after backing up both tables.
Backup both tables, copy missing rows into seo, and remove the duplicate field from templates?
Create Schema.org nodes in a database-backed builder. Pick a type, choose templates, then map schema properties to ProcessWire fields or source expressions.

This page will become the AI area for SEO suggestions, metadata drafts, schema help, and report summaries. It uses Context AI Gateway when available, with Ichiban OpenRouter settings as fallback, and attaches exported Context files to test requests.
The provider returned an empty message body. Try another model, increase Max tokens, or reduce the Context files sent with the request.
Find practical SEO fixes from Context data.
Using the attached Context export, give me 5 practical SEO improvements for the current site in concise bullets. Mention which pages, templates, or fields you used.
Draft titles and descriptions for priority pages.
Using the attached Context export, choose 5 priority pages or templates that need stronger metadata. For each one, draft an SEO title under 60 characters and a meta description under 155 characters.

Using the attached Context export and the existing ProcessWire templates/fields, suggest Schema.org types and field mappings for the most important page templates. Include source fields such as field:title or field:summary when possible.
Using the attached Context export, write a concise client-friendly SEO report summary: current site structure, biggest opportunities, risks, and next actions. Keep it suitable for a monthly report.
Use sources like field:title, field:summary|truncate:160, {legacy_field}, custom text, or empty string.
IndexNow key file written to the site root.
IndexNow key saved, but the key file could not be written. Create {key}.txt in the site root with the key as file content.

Checks a URL sample with Google URL Inspection API and groups non-indexed / problematic pages by coverage reason. Ichiban first uses Top Pages from Search Console for the last 28 days, then fills the sample with public ProcessWire pages sorted by path. This is a cached site scan, not Google’s full Page indexing aggregate report.
No indexing scan yet. Click Scan indexing issues to inspect Top Pages from Search Console first, then public ProcessWire pages if more URLs are needed.
No indexing issues found in the checked URL sample.
Controls the Open Graph object type shared to social networks.
Large image cards usually perform better for editorial and marketing pages.
Use a standard Schema.org type or pick one of the custom schemas created in SEO > Schemas.
Relative importance within this site. Most pages should stay around 0.5.
Crawler hint for how often the content usually changes.
